In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.

The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|ja|睦月}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Shokuyojijirishu|ja|色葉字類抄}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006年6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref> [[en:Layue]] [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk en]] [[gan:Layuehak]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:12月 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng chạp]] [[wuu:腊月]] [[za:Ndwenlab]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]] [[zh:农历十二月]]

[[Category:Lunar December]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Untranslated Pages]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[sn:Layue]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Deprecated qqs front]] [[Category:Deprecated qqs back]] [[Category:kk]] [[en:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[en:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:December (旧暦)]] [[Category:qq]] {{citation needed}} [[en:Layue]] {{citation needed}} {{Japanese Traditional Lunar Months}} [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk hak]] [[gan:十二月]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng mười hai]] [[wuu:农历十二月]] [[za:Ndwensapi]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]] [[zh:农历十二月]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] {{short description|Month of the Chinese calendar}} {{more citations needed |date=June 2024}} {{italic title}}
'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月}}) is the last month of the year in the [[Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month}}}}.
==Festival== *The [[Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th [[Qing dynasty|Qing]] emperor of [[China]], issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *[[Emperor Go-Uda]], the 91st [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]] of [[Japan]], ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *[[Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the founder and first ''[[shōgun]]'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *[[Uemura Masahisa]], a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *[[Imperial Noble Consort Shushen]], an Imperial concubine of the [[Tongzhi Emperor]], ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *[[Yue Fei]], Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *[[Lu You]], a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *[[Emperor Go-Hanazono]], the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *[[Lê Uy Mục]], the eighth king of the later [[Lê dynasty]] of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *[[Yi Gwang-sik]], a Korean politician and general during the [[Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

{{Lunar December}} '''Lunar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国}} and {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Great Cold|ja|大寒}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|ja|12月}} to early {{ill|February|ja|2月}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|ja|睦月}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Shokuyojijirishu|ja|色葉字類抄}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006年6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref> [[en:Layue]] [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk en]] [[gan:Layuehak]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:12月 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng chạp]] [[wuu:腊月]] [[za:Ndwenlab]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]] [[zh:农历十二月]]

[[Category:Lunar December]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Untranslated Pages]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[sn:Layue]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Deprecated qqs front]] [[Category:Deprecated qqs back]] [[Category:kk]] [[en:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[en:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:December (旧暦)]] [[Category:qq]] {{citation needed}} [[en:Layue]] {{citation needed}} {{Japanese Traditional Lunar Months}} [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk hak]] [[gan:十二月]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng mười hai]] [[wuu:农历十二月]] [[za:Ndwensapi]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]] [[zh:农历十二月]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] {{short description|Month of the Chinese calendar}} {{more citations needed |date=June 2024}} {{italic title}}
'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月}}) is the last month of the year in the [[Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month}}}}.
==Festival== *The [[Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th [[Qing dynasty|Qing]] emperor of [[China]], issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *[[Emperor Go-Uda]], the 91st [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]] of [[Japan]], ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *[[Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the founder and first ''[[shōgun]]'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *[[Uemura Masahisa]], a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *[[Imperial Noble Consort Shushen]], an Imperial concubine of the [[Tongzhi Emperor]], ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *[[Yue Fei]], Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *[[Lu You]], a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *[[Emperor Go-Hanazono]], the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *[[Lê Uy Mục]], the eighth king of the later [[Lê dynasty]] of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *[[Yi Gwang-sik]], a Korean politician and general during the [[Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

revision.text:
{{Lunar December}} '''Lunar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国}} and {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Great Cold|ja|大寒}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|ja|12月}} to early {{ill|February|ja|2月}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|ja|睦月}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Shokuyojijirishu|ja|色葉字類抄}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006年6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref> [[en:Layue]] [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk en]] [[gan:Layuehak]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:12月 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng chạp]] [[wuu:腊月]] [[za:Ndwenlab]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]] [[zh:农历十二月]]
== Shiwasu Dates == {| class="wikitable" |+ Shiwasu Dates ! Gregorian Year !! New Moon !! Last Day !! Number of Days |- | 2014 || 2015年{{ill|January 20|ja|1月20日}} || 2015年{{ill|February 18|ja|2月18日}} || 30 days |- | 2015 || 2016年{{ill|January 10|ja|1月10日}} || 2016年{{ill|February 7|ja|2月7日}} || 29 days |- | 2016 || {{ill|December 29|ja|12月29日}} || 2017年{{ill|January 27|ja|1月27日}} || 30 days |- | 2017 || 2018年{{ill|January 17|ja|1月17日}} || 2018年{{ill|February 15|ja|2月15日}} || 30 days |- | 2018 || 2019年{{ill|January 6|ja|1月6日}} || 2019年{{ill|February 4|ja|2月4日}} || 30 days |- | 2019 || {{ill|December 26|ja|12月26日}} || 2020年{{ill|January 24|ja|1月24日}} || 30 days |- | 2020 || 2021年{{ill|January 13|ja|1月13日}} || 2021年{{ill|February 11|ja|2月11日}} || 30 days |- | 2021 || 2022年{{ill|January 3|ja|1月3日}} || 2022年{{ill|January 31|ja|1月31日}} || 29 days |- | 2022 || {{ill|December 23|ja|12月23日}} || 2023年{{ill|January 21|ja|1月21日}} || 30 days |- | 2023 || 2024年{{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}} || 2024年{{ill|February 9|ja|2月9日}} || 30 days |- | 2024 || {{ill|December 31|ja|12月31日}} || 2025年{{ill|January 28|ja|1月28日}} || 29 days |- | 2025 || 2026年{{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日}} || 2026年{{ill|February 16|ja|2月16日}} || 29 days |}

'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月}}) is the last month of the year in the [[Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month}}}}.
==Festival== *The [[Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th [[Qing dynasty|Qing]] emperor of [[China]], issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *[[Emperor Go-Uda]], the 91st [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]] of [[Japan]], ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *[[Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the founder and first ''[[shōgun]]'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *[[Uemura Masahisa]], a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *[[Imperial Noble Consort Shushen]], an Imperial concubine of the [[Tongzhi Emperor]], ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *[[Yue Fei]], Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *[[Lu You]], a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *[[Emperor Go-Hanazono]], the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *[[Lê Uy Mục]], the eighth king of the later [[Lê dynasty]] of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *[[Yi Gwang-sik]], a Korean politician and general during the [[Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

{{Lunar December}} '''Lunar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Lunisolar|ja|旧暦}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国}} and {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Great Cold|ja|大寒}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|ja|12月}} to early {{ill|February|ja|2月}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|ja|睦月}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Shokuyojijirishu|ja|色葉字類抄}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006年6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref> [[en:Layue]] [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk en]] [[gan:Layuehak]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:12月 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng chạp]] [[wuu:腊月]] [[za:Ndwenlab]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]] [[zh:农历十二月]]
== Shiwasu Dates == {| class="wikitable" |+ Shiwasu Dates ! Gregorian Year !! New Moon !! Last Day !! Number of Days |- | 2014 || 2015年{{ill|January 20|ja|1月20日}} || 2015年{{ill|February 18|ja|2月18日}} || 30 days |- | 2015 || 2016年{{ill|January 10|ja|1月10日}} || 2016年{{ill|February 7|ja|2月7日}} || 29 days |- | 2016 || {{ill|December 29|ja|12月29日}} || 2017年{{ill|January 27|ja|1月27日}} || 30 days |- | 2017 || 2018年{{ill|January 17|ja|1月17日}} || 2018年{{ill|February 15|ja|2月15日}} || 30 days |- | 2018 || 2019年{{ill|January 6|ja|1月6日}} || 2019年{{ill|February 4|ja|2月4日}} || 30 days |- | 2019 || {{ill|December 26|ja|12月26日}} || 2020年{{ill|January 24|ja|1月24日}} || 30 days |- | 2020 || 2021年{{ill|January 13|ja|1月13日}} || 2021年{{ill|February 11|ja|2月11日}} || 30 days |- | 2021 || 2022年{{ill|January 3|ja|1月3日}} || 2022年{{ill|January 31|ja|1月31日}} || 29 days |- | 2022 || {{ill|December 23|ja|12月23日}} || 2023年{{ill|January 21|ja|1月21日}} || 30 days |- | 2023 || 2024年{{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}} || 2024年{{ill|February 9|ja|2月9日}} || 30 days |- | 2024 || {{ill|December 31|ja|12月31日}} || 2025年{{ill|January 28|ja|1月28日}} || 29 days |- | 2025 || 2026年{{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日}} || 2026年{{ill|February 16|ja|2月16日}} || 29 days |}

[[Category:Lunar December]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Untranslated Pages]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[sn:Layue]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Deprecated qqs front]] [[Category:Deprecated qqs back]] [[Category:kk]] [[en:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[en:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:December (旧暦)]] [[Category:qq]] {{citation needed}} [[en:Layue]] {{citation needed}} {{Japanese Traditional Lunar Months}} [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk hak]] [[gan:十二月]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng mười hai]] [[wuu:农历十二月]] [[za:Ndwensapi]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]] [[zh:农历十二月]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] {{short description|Month of the Chinese calendar}} {{more citations needed |date=June 2024}} {{italic title}}
'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月}}) is the last month of the year in the [[Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month}}}}.
==Festival== *The [[Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th [[Qing dynasty|Qing]] emperor of [[China]], issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *[[Emperor Go-Uda]], the 91st [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]] of [[Japan]], ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *[[Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the founder and first ''[[shōgun]]'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *[[Uemura Masahisa]], a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *[[Imperial Noble Consort Shushen]], an Imperial concubine of the [[Tongzhi Emperor]], ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *[[Yue Fei]], Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *[[Lu You]], a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *[[Emperor Go-Hanazono]], the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *[[Lê Uy Mục]], the eighth king of the later [[Lê dynasty]] of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *[[Yi Gwang-sik]], a Korean politician and general during the [[Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''
==References== {{reflist}}

Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref> [[en:Layue]] [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk en]] [[gan:Layuehak]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:12月 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng chạp]] [[wuu:腊月]] [[za:Ndwenlab]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]] [[zh:农历十二月]]

[[Category:Lunar December]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Untranslated Pages]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]][[sn:Layue]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Deprecated qqs front]] [[Category:Deprecated qqs back]] [[Category:kk]] [[en:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[en:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:December (旧暦)]] [[Category:qq]] {{citation needed}} [[en:Layue]] {{citation needed}} {{Japanese Traditional Lunar Months}} [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk hak]] [[gan:十二月]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng mười hai]] [[wuu:农历十二月]] [[za:Ndwensapi]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]] [[zh:农历十二月]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] {{short description|Month of the Chinese calendar}} {{more citations needed |date=June 2024}} {{italic title}}
'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月}}) is the last month of the year in the [[Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month}}}}.
==Festival== *The [[Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th [[Qing dynasty|Qing]] emperor of [[China]], issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *[[Emperor Go-Uda]], the 91st [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]] of [[Japan]], ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *[[Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the founder and first ''[[shōgun]]'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *[[Uemura Masahisa]], a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *[[Imperial Noble Consort Shushen]], an Imperial concubine of the [[Tongzhi Emperor]], ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *[[Yue Fei]], Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *[[Lu You]], a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *[[Emperor Go-Hanazono]], the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *[[Lê Uy Mục]], the eighth king of the later [[Lê dynasty]] of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *[[Yi Gwang-sik]], a Korean politician and general during the [[Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''
==References== {{reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Lunar December}} '''Lunar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国}} and {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Great Cold|ja|大寒}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|ja|12月}} to early {{ill|February|ja|2月}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|ja|睦月}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Shokuyojijirishu|ja|色葉字類抄}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006年6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref> [[en:Layue]] [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk en]] [[gan:Layuehak]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:12月 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng chạp]] [[wuu:腊月]] [[za:Ndwenlab]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]] [[zh:农历十二月]]
== Shiwasu Dates == {| class="wikitable" |+ Shiwasu Dates ! Gregorian Year !! New Moon !! Last Day !! Number of Days |- | 2014 || 2015年{{ill|January 20|ja|1月20日}} || 2015年{{ill|February 18|ja|2月18日}} || 30 days |- | 2015 || 2016年{{ill|January 10|ja|1月10日}} || 2016年{{ill|February 7|ja|2月7日}} || 29 days |- | 2016 || {{ill|December 29|ja|12月29日}} || 2017年{{ill|January 27|ja|1月27日}} || 30 days |- | 2017 || 2018年{{ill|January 17|ja|1月17日}} || 2018年{{ill|February 15|ja|2月15日}} || 30 days |- | 2018 || 2019年{{ill|January 6|ja|1月6日}} || 2019年{{ill|February 4|ja|2月4日}} || 30 days |- | 2019 || {{ill|December 26|ja|12月26日}} || 2020年{{ill|January 24|ja|1月24日}} || 30 days |- | 2020 || 2021年{{ill|January 13|ja|1月13日}} || 2021年{{ill|February 11|ja|2月11日}} || 30 days |- | 2021 || 2022年{{ill|January 3|ja|1月3日}} || 2022年{{ill|January 31|ja|1月31日}} || 29 days |- | 2022 || {{ill|December 23|ja|12月23日}} || 2023年{{ill|January 21|ja|1月21日}} || 30 days |- | 2023 || 2024年{{ill|January 11|ja|1月11日}} || 2024年{{ill|February 9|ja|2月9日}} || 30 days |- | 2024 || {{ill|December 31|ja|12月31日}} || 2025年{{ill|January 28|ja|1月28日}} || 29 days |- | 2025 || 2026年{{ill|January 19|ja|1月19日}} || 2026年{{ill|February 16|ja|2月16日}} || 29 days |}

'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月}}) is the last month of the year in the [[Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month}}}}.
==Festival== *The [[Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th [[Qing dynasty|Qing]] emperor of [[China]], issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *[[Emperor Go-Uda]], the 91st [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]] of [[Japan]], ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *[[Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the founder and first ''[[shōgun]]'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *[[Uemura Masahisa]], a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *[[Imperial Noble Consort Shushen]], an Imperial concubine of the [[Tongzhi Emperor]], ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *[[Yue Fei]], Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *[[Lu You]], a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *[[Emperor Go-Hanazono]], the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *[[Lê Uy Mục]], the eighth king of the later [[Lê dynasty]] of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *[[Yi Gwang-sik]], a Korean politician and general during the [[Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

{{Lunar December}} '''Lunar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国}} and {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Great Cold|ja|大寒}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|ja|12月}} to early {{ill|February|ja|2月}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|ja|睦月}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Shokuyojijirishu|ja|色葉字類抄}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006年6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref> [[en:Layue]] [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk en]] [[gan:Layuehak]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:12月 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng chạp]] [[wuu:腊月]] [[za:Ndwenlab]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]] [[zh:农历十二月]]

[[Category:Lunar December]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Layue]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Layue]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Deprecated 11s front]] [[Category:Deprecated 11s back]] [[Category:kk]] [[en:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:11]] [[en:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:December (旧暦)]] [[Category:11]] {{citation needed}} [[en:Layue]] {{citation needed}} {{Japanese Traditional Lunar Months}} [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk hak]] [[gan:十二月]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng mười hai]] [[wuu:农历十二月]] [[za:Ndwensapi]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]] [[zh:农历十二月]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Layue]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] {{short description|Month of the Chinese calendar}} {{more citations needed |date=June 2024}} {{italic title}}
'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月}}) is the last month of the year in the [[Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month}}}}.
==Festival== *The [[Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th [[Qing dynasty|Qing]] emperor of [[China]], issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *[[Emperor Go-Uda]], the 91st [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]] of [[Japan]], ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *[[Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the founder and first ''[[shōgun]]'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *[[Uemura Masahisa]], a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *[[Imperial Noble Consort Shushen]], an Imperial concubine of the [[Tongzhi Emperor]], ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *[[Yue Fei]], Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *[[Lu You]], a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *[[Emperor Go-Hanazono]], the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *[[Lê Uy Mục]], the eighth king of the later [[Lê dynasty]] of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *[[Yi Gwang-sik]], a Korean politician and general during the [[Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

{{Lunar December}} '''Lunar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国}} and {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Great Cold|ja|大寒}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|ja|12月}} to early {{ill|February|ja|2月}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|ja|睦月}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Shokuyojijirishu|ja|色葉字類抄}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006年6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref> [[en:Layue]] [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk en]] [[gan:Layuehak]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:12月 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng chạp]] [[wuu:腊月]] [[za:Ndwenlab]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]] [[zh:农历十二月]]

'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月}}) is the last month of the year in the [[Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month}}}}.
==Festival== *The [[Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th [[Qing dynasty|Qing]] emperor of [[China]], issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *[[Emperor Go-Uda]], the 91st [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]] of [[Japan]], ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *[[Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the founder and first ''[[shōgun]]'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *[[Uemura Masahisa]], a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *[[Imperial Noble Consort Shushen]], an Imperial concubine of the [[Tongzhi Emperor]], ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *[[Yue Fei]], Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *[[Lu You]], a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *[[Emperor Go-Hanazono]], the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *[[Lê Uy Mục]], the eighth king of the later [[Lê dynasty]] of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *[[Yi Gwang-sik]], a Korean politician and general during the [[Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

{{Lunar December}} '''Lunar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国}} and {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Great Cold|ja|大寒}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|ja|12月}} to early {{ill|February|ja|2月}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|ja|睦月}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Shokuyojijirishu|ja|色葉字類抄}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006年6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref> [[en:Layue]] [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk en]] [[gan:Layuehak]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:12月 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng chạp]] [[wuu:腊月]] [[za:Ndwenlab]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]] [[zh:农历十二月]]

{{Lunar December}} '''Lunar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国}} and {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Great Cold|ja|大寒}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|ja|12月}} to early {{ill|February|ja|2月}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|ja|睦月}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Shokuyojijirishu|ja|色葉字類抄}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006年6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref> [[en:Layue]] [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk en]] [[gan:Layuehak]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:12月 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng chạp]] [[wuu:腊月]] [[za:Ndwenlab]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]] [[zh:农历十二月]]

'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月}}) is the last month of the year in the [[Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month}}}}.
==Festival== *The [[Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th [[Qing dynasty|Qing]] emperor of [[China]], issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *[[Emperor Go-Uda]], the 91st [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]] of [[Japan]], ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *[[Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the founder and first ''[[shōgun]]'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *[[Uemura Masahisa]], a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *[[Imperial Noble Consort Shushen]], an Imperial concubine of the [[Tongzhi Emperor]], ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *[[Yue Fei]], Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *[[Lu You]], a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *[[Emperor Go-Hanazono]], the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *[[Lê Uy Mục]], the eighth king of the later [[Lê dynasty]] of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *[[Yi Gwang-sik]], a Korean politician and general during the [[Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

{{Lunar December}} '''Lunar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国}} and {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Great Cold|ja|大寒}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|ja|12月}} to early {{ill|February|ja|2月}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|ja|睦月}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Shokuyojijirishu|ja|色葉字類抄}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006年6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref> [[en:Layue]] [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk en]] [[gan:Layuehak]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:12月 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng chạp]] [[wuu:腊月]] [[za:Ndwenlab]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]] [[zh:农历十二月]]

Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Shokuyojijirishu|ja|色葉字類抄}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006年6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源}}).

Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref> [[en:Layue]] [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk en]] [[gan:Layuehak]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:12月 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng chạp]] [[wuu:腊月]] [[za:Ndwenlab]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]] [[zh:农历十二月]]

[[Category:Lunar December]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Layue]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Layue]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Deprecated 11s front]] [[Category:Deprecated 11s back]] [[en:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:11]] [[en:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:December (旧暦)]] [[Category:11]] {{citation needed}} [[en:Layue]] {{citation needed}} {{Japanese Traditional Lunar Months}} [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk hak]] [[gan:十二月]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng mười hai]] [[wuu:农历十二月]] [[za:Ndwensapi]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]] [[zh:农历十二月]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Layue]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] {{short description|Month of the Chinese calendar}} {{more citations needed |date=June 2024}} {{italic title}}
'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月}}) is the last month of the year in the [[Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month}}}}.
==Festival== *The [[Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th [[Qing dynasty|Qing]] emperor of [[China]], issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *[[Emperor Go-Uda]], the 91st [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]] of [[Japan]], ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *[[Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the founder and first ''[[shōgun]]'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *[[Uemura Masahisa]], a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *[[Imperial Noble Consort Shushen]], an Imperial concubine of the [[Tongzhi Emperor]], ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *[[Yue Fei]], Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *[[Lu You]], a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *[[Emperor Go-Hanazono]], the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *[[Lê Uy Mục]], the eighth king of the later [[Lê dynasty]] of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *[[Yi Gwang-sik]], a Korean politician and general during the [[Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

{{Lunar December}} '''Lunar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国}} and {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Great Cold|ja|大寒}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|ja|12月}} to early {{ill|February|ja|2月}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|ja|睦月}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Shokuyojijirishu|ja|色葉字類抄}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006年6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref> [[en:Layue]] [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk en]] [[gan:Layuehak]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:12月 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng chạp]] [[wuu:腊月]] [[za:Ndwenlab]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]] [[zh:农历十二月]]

{{Lunar December}} '''Lunar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国}} and {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}.

In the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|ja|12月}} to early {{ill|February|ja|2月}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.

[[Category:Lunar December]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Layue]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Layue]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Deprecated 11s front]] [[Category:Deprecated 11s back]] [[en:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:11]] [[en:旧暦十二月]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:December (旧暦)]] [[Category:11]] {{citation needed}} [[en:Layue]] {{citation needed}} {{Japanese Traditional Lunar Months}} [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk hak]] [[gan:十二月]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng mười hai]] [[wuu:农历十二月]] [[za:Ndwensapi]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]] [[zh:农历十二月]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Layue]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] {{short description|Month of the Chinese calendar}} {{more citations needed |date=June 2024}} {{italic title}}
'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月}}) is the last month of the year in the [[Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month}}}}.
==Festival== *The [[Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th [[Qing dynasty|Qing]] emperor of [[China]], issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *[[Emperor Go-Uda]], the 91st [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]] of [[Japan]], ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *[[Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the founder and first ''[[shōgun]]'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *[[Uemura Masahisa]], a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *[[Imperial Noble Consort Shushen]], an Imperial concubine of the [[Tongzhi Emperor]], ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *[[Yue Fei]], Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *[[Lu You]], a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *[[Emperor Go-Hanazono]], the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *[[Lê Uy Mục]], the eighth king of the later [[Lê dynasty]] of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *[[Yi Gwang-sik]], a Korean politician and general during the [[Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

{{Lunar December}} '''Lunar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国}} and {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Great Cold|ja|大寒}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|ja|12月}} to early {{ill|February|ja|2月}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|ja|睦月}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Shokuyojijirishu|ja|色葉字類抄}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006年6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref> [[en:Layue]] [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk en]] [[gan:Layuehak]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:12月 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng chạp]] [[wuu:腊月]] [[za:Ndwenlab]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]] [[zh:农历十二月]]

'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月}}) is the last month of the year in the [[Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month}}}}.
==Festival== *The [[Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th [[Qing dynasty|Qing]] emperor of [[China]], issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *[[Emperor Go-Uda]], the 91st [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]] of [[Japan]], ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *[[Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the founder and first ''[[shōgun]]'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *[[Uemura Masahisa]], a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *[[Imperial Noble Consort Shushen]], an Imperial concubine of the [[Tongzhi Emperor]], ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *[[Yue Fei]], Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *[[Lu You]], a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *[[Emperor Go-Hanazono]], the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *[[Lê Uy Mục]], the eighth king of the later [[Lê dynasty]] of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *[[Yi Gwang-sik]], a Korean politician and general during the [[Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

{{Lunar December}} '''Lunar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国}} and {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Great Cold|ja|大寒}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|ja|12月}} to early {{ill|February|ja|2月}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|ja|睦月}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Shokuyojijirishu|ja|色葉字類抄}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006年6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref> [[en:Layue]] [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk en]] [[gan:Layuehak]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:12月 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng chạp]] [[wuu:腊月]] [[za:Ndwenlab]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]] [[zh:农历十二月]]

{{Lunar December}} '''Lunar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国}} and {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Great Cold|ja|大寒}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|ja|12月}} to early {{ill|February|ja|2月}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|ja|睦月}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Shokuyojijirishu|ja|色葉字類抄}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006年6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref> [[en:Layue]] [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk en]] [[gan:Layuehak]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:12月 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng chạp]] [[wuu:腊月]] [[za:Ndwenlab]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]] [[zh:农历十二月]]

'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月}}) is the last month of the year in the [[Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month}}}}.
==Festival== *The [[Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th [[Qing dynasty|Qing]] emperor of [[China]], issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *[[Emperor Go-Uda]], the 91st [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]] of [[Japan]], ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *[[Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the founder and first ''[[shōgun]]'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *[[Uemura Masahisa]], a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *[[Imperial Noble Consort Shushen]], an Imperial concubine of the [[Tongzhi Emperor]], ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *[[Yue Fei]], Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *[[Lu You]], a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *[[Emperor Go-Hanazono]], the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *[[Lê Uy Mục]], the eighth king of the later [[Lê dynasty]] of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *[[Yi Gwang-sik]], a Korean politician and general during the [[Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

{{Lunar December}} '''Lunar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国}} and {{ill|Japan|ja|日本}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Great Cold|ja|大寒}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|ja|12月}} to early {{ill|February|ja|2月}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|ja|睦月}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Shokuyojijirishu|ja|色葉字類抄}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006年6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref> [[en:Layue]] [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk en]] [[gan:Layuehak]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]] [[ja:12月 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng chạp]] [[wuu:腊月]] [[za:Ndwenlab]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]] [[zh:农历十二月]]
