{{Lunisolar December}} '''Lunisolar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Japan|ja|日本|12=draft|12=draft}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft|12=draft}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=draft|12=draft}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Great Cold|ja|大寒|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|ja|12月|12=draft|12=draft}} to early {{ill|February|ja|2月|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=draft|12=draft}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.

The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|12=draft|12=draft}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|ja|睦月|12=draft|12=draft}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Shokuyojijirishu|ja|色葉字類抄|12=draft|12=draft}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|12=draft|12=draft}}).

[[wuu:农历十二月]]
[[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]]
{{short description|Month of the Chinese calendar}} {{more citations needed |date=June 2024}} {{italic title}}
'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月}}) is the last month of the year in the [[Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month}}}}.
==Festival== *The [[Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th [[Qing dynasty|Qing]] emperor of [[China]], issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *[[Emperor Go-Uda]], the 91st [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]] of [[Japan]], ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *[[Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the founder and first ''[[shōgun]]'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *[[Uemura Masahisa]], a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *[[Imperial Noble Consort Shushen]], an Imperial concubine of the [[Tongzhi Emperor]], ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *[[Yue Fei]], Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *[[Lu You]], a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *[[Emperor Go-Hanazono]], the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *[[Lê Uy Mục]], the eighth king of the later [[Lê dynasty]] of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *[[Yi Gwang-sik]], a Korean politician and general during the [[Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''
==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Lunisolar December]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Deprecated 11s back]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Chinese calendars]] [[Category:Chinese words and phrases]] [[Category:Lunisolar months]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]] [[Category:Lunisolar]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:Layue]] [[ja:12月 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng chạp]] [[za:Ndwenlab]] [[zh:农历十二月]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] [[sn:Layue]] [[en:旧暦十二月]] [[sn:旧暦十二月]] [[sn:December (旧暦)]] [[vi:Tháng mười hai]] [[za:Ndwensapi]] [[sn:ayue]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Layue}}

{{Lunisolar December}} '''Lunisolar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Japan|ja|日本|12=draft|12=draft}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft|12=draft}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=draft|12=draft}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Great Cold|ja|大寒|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|ja|12月|12=draft|12=draft}} to early {{ill|February|ja|2月|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=draft|12=draft}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|12=draft|12=draft}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|ja|睦月|12=draft|12=draft}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Shokuyojijirishu|ja|色葉字類抄|12=draft|12=draft}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|12=draft|12=draft}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref>

'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月}}) is the last month of the year in the [[Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month}}}}.
==Festival== *The [[Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th [[Qing dynasty|Qing]] emperor of [[China]], issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *[[Emperor Go-Uda]], the 91st [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]] of [[Japan]], ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *[[Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the founder and first ''[[shōgun]]'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *[[Uemura Masahisa]], a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *[[Imperial Noble Consort Shushen]], an Imperial concubine of the [[Tongzhi Emperor]], ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *[[Yue Fei]], Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *[[Lu You]], a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *[[Emperor Go-Hanazono]], the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *[[Lê Uy Mục]], the eighth king of the later [[Lê dynasty]] of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *[[Yi Gwang-sik]], a Korean politician and general during the [[Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

revision.text:
{{Lunisolar December}} '''Lunisolar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Japan|ja|日本|12=draft|12=draft}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft|12=draft}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=draft|12=draft}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Great Cold|ja|大寒|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|ja|12月|12=draft|12=draft}} to early {{ill|February|ja|2月|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=draft|12=draft}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|12=draft|12=draft}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|ja|睦月|12=draft|12=draft}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Shokuyojijirishu|ja|色葉字類抄|12=draft|12=draft}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|12=draft|12=draft}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref>

'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月}}) is the last month of the year in the [[Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month}}}}.
==Festival== *The [[Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th [[Qing dynasty|Qing]] emperor of [[China]], issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *[[Emperor Go-Uda]], the 91st [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]] of [[Japan]], ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *[[Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the founder and first ''[[shōgun]]'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *[[Uemura Masahisa]], a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *[[Imperial Noble Consort Shushen]], an Imperial concubine of the [[Tongzhi Emperor]], ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *[[Yue Fei]], Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *[[Lu You]], a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *[[Emperor Go-Hanazono]], the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *[[Lê Uy Mục]], the eighth king of the later [[Lê dynasty]] of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *[[Yi Gwang-sik]], a Korean politician and general during the [[Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

revision.text:
{{Lunisolar December}} '''Lunisolar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Japan|ja|日本|12=draft|12=draft}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft|12=draft}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=draft|12=draft}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Great Cold|ja|大寒|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|ja|12月|12=draft|12=draft}} to early {{ill|February|ja|2月|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=draft|12=draft}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|12=draft|12=draft}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|ja|睦月|12=draft|12=draft}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Shokuyojijirishu|ja|色葉字類抄|12=draft|12=draft}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|12=draft|12=draft}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref>
[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk en]] [[gan:Layuehak]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]]
[[wuu:腊月]]

{{short description|Month of the Chinese calendar}} {{more citations needed |date=June 2024}} {{italic title}}
'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月}}) is the last month of the year in the [[Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month}}}}.
==Festival== *The [[Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th [[Qing dynasty|Qing]] emperor of [[China]], issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *[[Emperor Go-Uda]], the 91st [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]] of [[Japan]], ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *[[Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the founder and first ''[[shōgun]]'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *[[Uemura Masahisa]], a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *[[Imperial Noble Consort Shushen]], an Imperial concubine of the [[Tongzhi Emperor]], ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *[[Yue Fei]], Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *[[Lu You]], a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *[[Emperor Go-Hanazono]], the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *[[Lê Uy Mục]], the eighth king of the later [[Lê dynasty]] of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *[[Yi Gwang-sik]], a Korean politician and general during the [[Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

{{Lunisolar December}} '''Lunisolar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Japan|ja|日本|12=draft|12=draft}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft|12=draft}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=draft|12=draft}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Great Cold|ja|大寒|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|ja|12月|12=draft|12=draft}} to early {{ill|February|ja|2月|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=draft|12=draft}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|12=draft|12=draft}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|ja|睦月|12=draft|12=draft}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Shokuyojijirishu|ja|色葉字類抄|12=draft|12=draft}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|12=draft|12=draft}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref>

'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月}}) is the last month of the year in the [[Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month}}}}.
==Festival== *The [[Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.

{{Lunisolar December}} '''Lunisolar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Japan|ja|日本|12=draft|12=draft}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft|12=draft}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=draft|12=draft}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō Calendar|ja|天保暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Great Cold|ja|大寒|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian Calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|ja|12月|12=draft|12=draft}} to early {{ill|February|ja|2月|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|ja|太陰太陽暦|12=draft|12=draft}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=draft|12=draft}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|12=draft|12=draft}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|ja|睦月|12=draft|12=draft}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Shokuyojijirishu|ja|色葉字類抄|12=draft|12=draft}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|12=draft|12=draft}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref>

{{citation needed}} {{Japanese Traditional Lunisolar Months}} [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk hak]] [[gan:十二月]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍tja]]
[[wuu:农历十二月]]
[[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:十二月]]
{{short description|Month of the Chinese calendar}} {{more citations needed |date=June 2024}} {{italic title}}
'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月}}) is the last month of the year in the [[Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month}}}}.
==Festival== *The [[Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th [[Qing dynasty|Qing]] emperor of [[China]], issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *[[Emperor Go-Uda]], the 91st [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]] of [[Japan]], ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *[[Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the founder and first ''[[shōgun]]'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *[[Uemura Masahisa]], a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *[[Imperial Noble Consort Shushen]], an Imperial concubine of the [[Tongzhi Emperor]], ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *[[Yue Fei]], Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *[[Lu You]], a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *[[Emperor Go-Hanazono]], the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *[[Lê Uy Mục]], the eighth king of the later [[Lê dynasty]] of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *[[Yi Gwang-sik]], a Korean politician and general during the [[Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''
==References== {{reflist}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Lunisolar December]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Deprecated 11s back]] [[Category:Expanded pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:Chinese calendars]] [[Category:Chinese words and phrases]] [[Category:Lunisolar months]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]] [[Category:Lunisolar]] [[Category:New]] }} [[en:Layue]] [[ja:12月 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 12월]] [[th:เดือน 12]] [[vi:Tháng chạp]] [[za:Ndwenlab]] [[zh:农历十二月]] [[ja:旧暦十二月]] [[sn:Layue]] [[en:旧暦十二月]] [[sn:旧暦十二月]] [[sn:December (旧暦)]] [[vi:Tháng mười hai]] [[za:Ndwensapi]] [[sn:ayue]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Layue}} [[ar:Layue]]

revision.text:
'''旧暦12月'''（きゅうれきじゅうにがつ）は、[[太陰太陽暦]]である[[中国]]、[[日本]]の[[旧暦]]の年初から12番目の[[月 (暦)|月]]である。
[[天保暦]]よりも前の[[和暦]]などでは、[[大寒]]を含む月を12月とする。[[グレゴリオ暦]]では[[12月]]下旬から[[2月]]上旬ごろに当たる。東洋の[[太陰太陽暦]]では月の日数である[[大小]]（大月30日、小月29日）が年により異なるため、月の末日が29日である年もある。
== 別名・異名 == ;しはすの語源 12月の別名は'''師走'''（しはす、しわす）である。「しはす」の語源は、古いことなので分からない。「[[大言海]]」は、「歳極（トシハツ）ノ略轉カト云フ、或ハ、萬事爲果（シハ）つ月ノ意、又、農事終ハル意カ」と言い<ref>[http://www.babelbible.net/genkai/genkai.cgi?mode=right&imode=0&noexpand=0&page=461 WEB言海] p.461、「志はす」の項（「大言海」とは、やや記述が異なる）</ref>、また「十二箇月ノ名ハ、スベテ稻禾生熟ノ次第ヲ逐ヒテ、名ヅケシナリ」（「睦月」の項）と言っている<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.87、文春文庫、第1刷、2006年6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref>。なお、「僧侶（師は、僧侶の意）が仏事で走り回る忙しさから」、という平安時代からの説（[[色葉字類抄]]に記述がある。）があるが、これは[[語源俗解]]（[[言語学]]的な根拠がない、あてずっぽうの語源のこと）であり、平安時代にはすでに、「しはす」の語源は分からなくなっていたのである{{Sfn|三省堂|2015}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006年6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref>（[[民間語源#日本語における民間語源]]）。
;その他 異名で陰暦12月を「きとう（季冬）」という。[[臘]]の月にちなみ「臘月」ともいう<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref>。
== 師走の日付 == {| class="wikitable" |+ 師走の日付 !西暦!!朔!!晦!!日数 |- |2014年||2015年[[1月20日]]||2015年[[2月18日]]||30日 |- |2015年||2016年[[1月10日]]||2016年[[2月7日]]||29日 |- |2016年||[[12月29日]]||2017年[[1月27日]]||30日 |- |2017年||2018年[[1月17日]]||2018年[[2月15日]]||30日 |- |2018年||2019年[[1月6日]]||2019年[[2月4日]]||30日 |- |2019年||[[12月26日]]||2020年[[1月24日]]||30日 |- |2020年||2021年[[1月13日]]||2021年[[2月11日]]||30日 |- |2021年||2022年[[1月3日]]||2022年[[1月31日]]||29日 |- |2022年||[[12月23日]]||2023年[[1月21日]]||30日 |- |2023年||2024年[[1月11日]]||2024年[[2月9日]]||30日 |- |2024年||[[12月31日]]||2025年[[1月28日]]||29日 |- |2025年||2026年[[1月19日]]||2026年[[2月16日]]||29日 |}
== 引用 == <references />
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|url=https://www.sanseido-publ.co.jp/publ/gen/gen2lang/nazenanij/pdf/sample386-387.pdf|chapter=「師走」に師は走らなかった？！|author=関根健一|title=なぜなに日本語|date=2015-05-25|publisher=三省堂|isbn=978-4-385-36606-7|pages=386-387|format=PDF|ref={{SfnRef|三省堂|2015}}}}
==外部リンク== * [https://gogen-yurai.jp/shiwasu/ 師走 - 語源由来辞典]
== 関連項目 == * [[臘八節]] {{月 (暦)|旧暦12月|旧暦の日付の一覧|月 (旧暦)|12|日 (旧暦)|[[12月30日 (旧暦)|30]]}} [[Category:旧暦12月|*]]

[[天保暦]]よりも前の[[和暦]]などでは、[[大寒]]を含む月を12月とする。[[グレゴリオ暦]]では[[12月]]下旬から[[2月]]上旬ごろに当たる。東洋の[[太陰太陽暦]]では月の日数である[[大小]]（大月30日、小月29日）が年により異なるため、月の末日が29日である年もある。
== 別名・異名 == ;しはすの語源 12月の別名は'''師走'''（しはす、しわす）である。「しはす」の語源は、古いことなので分からない。「[[大言海]]」は、「歳極（トシハツ）ノ略轉カト云フ、或ハ、萬事爲果（シハ）つ月ノ意、又、農事終ハル意カ」と言い<ref>[http://www.babelbible.net/genkai/genkai.cgi?mode=right&imode=0&noexpand=0&page=461 WEB言海] p.461、「志はす」の項（「大言海」とは、やや記述が異なる）</ref>、また「十二箇月ノ名ハ、スベテ稻禾生熟ノ次第ヲ逐ヒテ、名ヅケシナリ」（「睦月」の項）と言っている<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.87、文春文庫、第1刷、2006年6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref>。なお、「僧侶（師は、僧侶の意）が仏事で走り回る忙しさから」、という平安時代からの説（[[色葉字類抄]]に記述がある。）があるが、これは[[語源俗解]]（[[言語学]]的な根拠がない、あてずっぽうの語源のこと）であり、平安時代にはすでに、「しはす」の語源は分からなくなっていたのである{{Sfn|三省堂|2015}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006年6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref>（[[民間語源#日本語における民間語源]]）。
;その他 異名で陰暦12月を「きとう（季冬）」という。[[臘]]の月にちなみ「臘月」ともいう<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja}}</ref>。
== 師走の日付 == {| class="wikitable" |+ 師走の日付 !西暦!!朔!!晦!!日数 |- |2014年||2015年[[1月20日]]||2015年[[2月18日]]||30日 |- |2015年||2016年[[1月10日]]||2016年[[2月7日]]||29日 |- |2016年||[[12月29日]]||2017年[[1月27日]]||30日 |- |2017年||2018年[[1月17日]]||2018年[[2月15日]]||30日 |- |2018年||2019年[[1月6日]]||2019年[[2月4日]]||30日 |- |2019年||[[12月26日]]||2020年[[1月24日]]||30日 |- |2020年||2021年[[1月13日]]||2021年[[2月11日]]||30日 |- |2021年||2022年[[1月3日]]||2022年[[1月31日]]||29日 |- |2022年||[[12月23日]]||2023年[[1月21日]]||30日 |- |2023年||2024年[[1月11日]]||2024年[[2月9日]]||30日 |- |2024年||[[12月31日]]||2025年[[1月28日]]||29日 |- |2025年||2026年[[1月19日]]||2026年[[2月16日]]||29日 |}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q905470]] Wikidata name: 12th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar December|qq=}} '''Lunisolar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}} and [[:en:Japan|Japan]], based on the [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]].
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|qq=|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5372133}} prior to the [[:en:Tenpō calendar|Tenpō Calendar]], December is defined as the month that includes [[:en:Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]]. In the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]], it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:December|December]] to early [[:en:February|February]]. In the Eastern [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|qq=|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigenkai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 言海|1=Genkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630981}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the [[:en:Mutsuki|Mutsuki]] entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in [[:en:Iroha Jiruishō|Shokuyojijirishu]]; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015|qq=}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Etymology|qq=|1=folk etymology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109964}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Là|qq=|1=Là|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612428}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref>

== Related Articles == * [[:en:Laba Festival|Laba Festival]] {{Month (Calendar)|Lunisolar December|List of Lunisolar Dates|Month (Lunisolar)|12|Day (Lunisolar)|{{ill|December 30 (Lunisolar)|ja|12月30日 (旧暦)|lt=30|lt_ja=30|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 30 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|qq=}}
{{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} {{short description|Month of the Chinese calendar|qq=}} {{more citations needed |date=June 2024|qq=}} {{italic title|qq=}}
'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月|qq=}}) is the last month of the year in the [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[:en:Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[:en:Solar term|Solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month|qq=}}|qq=}}.
==Festival== *The [[:en:Laba Festival|Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily|qq=}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日|qq=}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年|qq=}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕|qq=}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[:en:Aisin-Gioro Puyi|Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th [[:en:Qing dynasty|Qing]] emperor of [[:en:China|China]], issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *[[:en:Emperor Go-Uda|Emperor Go-Uda]], the 91st [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperor]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]], ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *[[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the founder and first ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *[[:en:Uemura Masahisa|Uemura Masahisa]], a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *[[:en:Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen]], an Imperial concubine of the [[:en:Tongzhi Emperor|Tongzhi Emperor]], ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *[[:en:Yue Fei|Yue Fei]], Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[:en:Southern Song dynasty|Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *[[:en:Lu You|Lu You]], a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *[[:en:Emperor Go-Hanazono|Emperor Go-Hanazono]], the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *[[:en:Lê Uy Mục|Lê Uy Mục]], the eighth king of the later [[:en:Lê dynasty|Lê dynasty]] of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *[[:en:Yi Gwang-sik|Yi Gwang-sik]], a Korean politician and general during the [[:en:Joseon dynasty|Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

In the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]], it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:December|December]] to early [[:en:February|February]]. In the Eastern [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|qq=|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigenkai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 言海|1=Genkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630981}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the [[:en:Mutsuki|Mutsuki]] entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in [[:en:Iroha Jiruishō|Shokuyojijirishu]]; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015|qq=}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Etymology|qq=|1=folk etymology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109964}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Là|qq=|1=Là|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612428}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref>

In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|qq=|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5372133}} prior to the [[:en:Tenpō calendar|Tenpō Calendar]], December is defined as the month that includes [[:en:Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]]. In the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]], it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:December|December]] to early [[:en:February|February]]. In the Eastern [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|qq=|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigenkai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 言海|1=Genkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630981}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the [[:en:Mutsuki|Mutsuki]] entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in [[:en:Iroha Jiruishō|Shokuyojijirishu]]; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015|qq=}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Etymology|qq=|1=folk etymology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109964}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Là|qq=|1=Là|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612428}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref>

== Related Articles == * [[:en:Laba Festival|Laba Festival]] {{Month (Calendar)|Lunisolar December|List of Lunisolar Dates|Month (Lunisolar)|12|Day (Lunisolar)|{{ill|December 30 (Lunisolar)|ja|12月30日 (旧暦)|lt=30|lt_ja=30|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 30 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|qq=}}
{{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} {{short description|Month of the Chinese calendar|qq=}} {{more citations needed |date=June 2024|qq=}} {{italic title|qq=}}
'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月|qq=}}) is the last month of the year in the [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[:en:Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[:en:Solar term|Solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month|qq=}}|qq=}}.
==Festival== *The [[:en:Laba Festival|Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily|qq=}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日|qq=}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年|qq=}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕|qq=}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[:en:Aisin-Gioro Puyi|Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th [[:en:Qing dynasty|Qing]] emperor of [[:en:China|China]], issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *[[:en:Emperor Go-Uda|Emperor Go-Uda]], the 91st [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperor]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]], ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *[[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the founder and first ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *[[:en:Uemura Masahisa|Uemura Masahisa]], a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *[[:en:Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen]], an Imperial concubine of the [[:en:Tongzhi Emperor|Tongzhi Emperor]], ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *[[:en:Yue Fei|Yue Fei]], Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[:en:Southern Song dynasty|Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *[[:en:Lu You|Lu You]], a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *[[:en:Emperor Go-Hanazono|Emperor Go-Hanazono]], the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *[[:en:Lê Uy Mục|Lê Uy Mục]], the eighth king of the later [[:en:Lê dynasty|Lê dynasty]] of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *[[:en:Yi Gwang-sik|Yi Gwang-sik]], a Korean politician and general during the [[:en:Joseon dynasty|Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

In the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]], it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:December|December]] to early [[:en:February|February]]. In the Eastern [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|qq=|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigenkai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 言海|1=Genkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630981}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the [[:en:Mutsuki|Mutsuki]] entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in [[:en:Iroha Jiruishō|Shokuyojijirishu]]; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015|qq=}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Etymology|qq=|1=folk etymology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109964}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Là|qq=|1=Là|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612428}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref>

In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|qq=|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5372133}} prior to the [[:en:Tenpō calendar|Tenpō Calendar]], December is defined as the month that includes [[:en:Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]]. In the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]], it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:December|December]] to early [[:en:February|February]]. In the Eastern [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|qq=|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigenkai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 言海|1=Genkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630981}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the [[:en:Mutsuki|Mutsuki]] entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in [[:en:Iroha Jiruishō|Shokuyojijirishu]]; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015|qq=}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Etymology|qq=|1=folk etymology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109964}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Là|qq=|1=Là|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612428}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref>

== Related Articles == * [[:en:Laba Festival|Laba Festival]] {{Month (Calendar)|Lunisolar December|List of Lunisolar Dates|Month (Lunisolar)|12|Day (Lunisolar)|{{ill|December 30 (Lunisolar)|ja|12月30日 (旧暦)|lt=30|lt_ja=30|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 30 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|qq=}}
{{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} {{short description|Month of the Chinese calendar|qq=}} {{more citations needed |date=June 2024|qq=}} {{italic title|qq=}}
'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月|qq=}}) is the last month of the year in the [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[:en:Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[:en:Solar term|Solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month|qq=}}|qq=}}.
==Festival== *The [[:en:Laba Festival|Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily|qq=}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日|qq=}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年|qq=}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕|qq=}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[:en:Aisin-Gioro Puyi|Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th [[:en:Qing dynasty|Qing]] emperor of [[:en:China|China]], issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *[[:en:Emperor Go-Uda|Emperor Go-Uda]], the 91st [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperor]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]], ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *[[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the founder and first ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *[[:en:Uemura Masahisa|Uemura Masahisa]], a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *[[:en:Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen]], an Imperial concubine of the [[:en:Tongzhi Emperor|Tongzhi Emperor]], ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *[[:en:Yue Fei|Yue Fei]], Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[:en:Southern Song dynasty|Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *[[:en:Lu You|Lu You]], a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *[[:en:Emperor Go-Hanazono|Emperor Go-Hanazono]], the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *[[:en:Lê Uy Mục|Lê Uy Mục]], the eighth king of the later [[:en:Lê dynasty|Lê dynasty]] of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *[[:en:Yi Gwang-sik|Yi Gwang-sik]], a Korean politician and general during the [[:en:Joseon dynasty|Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

In the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]], it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:December|December]] to early [[:en:February|February]]. In the Eastern [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|qq=|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigenkai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 言海|1=Genkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630981}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the [[:en:Mutsuki|Mutsuki]] entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in [[:en:Iroha Jiruishō|Shokuyojijirishu]]; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015|qq=}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Etymology|qq=|1=folk etymology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109964}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Là|qq=|1=Là|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612428}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref>

In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5372133}} prior to the [[:en:Tenpō calendar|Tenpō Calendar]], December is defined as the month that includes [[:en:Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]]. In the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]], it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:December|December]] to early [[:en:February|February]]. In the Eastern [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigenkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 言海|1=Genkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630981}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the [[:en:Mutsuki|Mutsuki]] entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in [[:en:Iroha Jiruishō|Shokuyojijirishu]]; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015|qq=}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Etymology|1=folk etymology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109964}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Là|1=Là|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612428}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref>

== Related Articles == * [[:en:Laba Festival|Laba Festival]] {{Month (Calendar)|Lunisolar December|List of Lunisolar Dates|Month (Lunisolar)|12|Day (Lunisolar)|{{ill|December 30 (Lunisolar)|ja|12月30日 (旧暦)|lt=30|lt_ja=30|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 30 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|qq=}}
{{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} {{short description|Month of the Chinese calendar|qq=}} {{more citations needed |date=June 2024|qq=}} {{italic title|qq=}}
'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月|qq=}}) is the last month of the year in the [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[:en:Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[:en:Solar term|Solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month|qq=}}|qq=}}.
==Festival== *The [[:en:Laba Festival|Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily|qq=}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日|qq=}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年|qq=}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕|qq=}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[:en:Aisin-Gioro Puyi|Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th [[:en:Qing dynasty|Qing]] emperor of [[:en:China|China]], issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *[[:en:Emperor Go-Uda|Emperor Go-Uda]], the 91st [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperor]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]], ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *[[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the founder and first ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *[[:en:Uemura Masahisa|Uemura Masahisa]], a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *[[:en:Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen]], an Imperial concubine of the [[:en:Tongzhi Emperor|Tongzhi Emperor]], ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *[[:en:Yue Fei|Yue Fei]], Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[:en:Southern Song dynasty|Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *[[:en:Lu You|Lu You]], a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *[[:en:Emperor Go-Hanazono|Emperor Go-Hanazono]], the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *[[:en:Lê Uy Mục|Lê Uy Mục]], the eighth king of the later [[:en:Lê dynasty|Lê dynasty]] of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *[[:en:Yi Gwang-sik|Yi Gwang-sik]], a Korean politician and general during the [[:en:Joseon dynasty|Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigenkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 言海|1=Genkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630981}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the [[:en:Mutsuki|Mutsuki]] entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in [[:en:Iroha Jiruishō|Shokuyojijirishu]]; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015|qq=}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Etymology|1=folk etymology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109964}}).

In the Eastern [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigenkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 言海|1=Genkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630981}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the [[:en:Mutsuki|Mutsuki]] entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in [[:en:Iroha Jiruishō|Shokuyojijirishu]]; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015|qq=}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Etymology|1=folk etymology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109964}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Là|1=Là|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612428}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref>

== Related Articles == * [[:en:Laba Festival|Laba Festival]] {{Month (Calendar)|Lunisolar December|List of Lunisolar Dates|Month (Lunisolar)|12|Day (Lunisolar)|{{ill|December 30 (Lunisolar)|ja|12月30日 (旧暦)|lt=30|lt_ja=30|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 30 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|qq=}}
{{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} {{short description|Month of the Chinese calendar|qq=}} {{more citations needed |date=June 2024|qq=}} {{italic title|qq=}}
'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月|qq=}}) is the last month of the year in the [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[:en:Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[:en:Solar term|Solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month|qq=}}|qq=}}.
==Festival== *The [[:en:Laba Festival|Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily|qq=}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日|qq=}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年|qq=}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕|qq=}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[:en:Aisin-Gioro Puyi|Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th [[:en:Qing dynasty|Qing]] emperor of [[:en:China|China]], issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *[[:en:Emperor Go-Uda|Emperor Go-Uda]], the 91st [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperor]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]], ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *[[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the founder and first ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *[[:en:Uemura Masahisa|Uemura Masahisa]], a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *[[:en:Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen]], an Imperial concubine of the [[:en:Tongzhi Emperor|Tongzhi Emperor]], ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *[[:en:Yue Fei|Yue Fei]], Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[:en:Southern Song dynasty|Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *[[:en:Lu You|Lu You]], a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *[[:en:Emperor Go-Hanazono|Emperor Go-Hanazono]], the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *[[:en:Lê Uy Mục|Lê Uy Mục]], the eighth king of the later [[:en:Lê dynasty|Lê dynasty]] of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *[[:en:Yi Gwang-sik|Yi Gwang-sik]], a Korean politician and general during the [[:en:Joseon dynasty|Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk]][[en:Layue]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍t]][[ja:12月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 12월]][[th:เดือน 12]][[vi:Tháng chạp]][[wuu:腊月]][[za:Ndwenlab]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十二月]][[zh:农历十二月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q905470]] Wikidata name: 12th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar December|qq=}} '''Lunisolar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}} and [[:en:Japan|Japan]], based on the [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]].
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5372133}} prior to the [[:en:Tenpō calendar|Tenpō Calendar]], December is defined as the month that includes [[:en:Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]]. In the [[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]], it corresponds approximately to late [[:en:December|December]] to early [[:en:February|February]]. In the Eastern [[:en:Lunisolar calendar|Lunisolar calendar]], because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigenkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 言海|1=Genkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630981}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the [[:en:Mutsuki|Mutsuki]] entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in [[:en:Iroha Jiruishō|Shokuyojijirishu]]; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015|qq=}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Etymology|1=folk etymology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109964}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Là|1=Là|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612428}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref>

'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月|qq=}}) is the last month of the year in the [[:en:Chinese calendar|Chinese calendar]]. In general, the [[:en:Dahan (solar term)|Great Cold]], the 24th [[:en:Solar term|Solar term]], is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month|qq=}}|qq=}}.
==Festival== *The [[:en:Laba Festival|Laba Festival]] is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily|qq=}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日|qq=}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年|qq=}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕|qq=}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[:en:Aisin-Gioro Puyi|Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th [[:en:Qing dynasty|Qing]] emperor of [[:en:China|China]], issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *[[:en:Emperor Go-Uda|Emperor Go-Uda]], the 91st [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperor]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]], ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *[[:en:Tokugawa Ieyasu|Tokugawa Ieyasu]], the founder and first ''[[:en:Shogun|Shogun]]'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *[[:en:Uemura Masahisa|Uemura Masahisa]], a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *[[:en:Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen]], an Imperial concubine of the [[:en:Tongzhi Emperor|Tongzhi Emperor]], ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *[[:en:Yue Fei|Yue Fei]], Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[:en:Southern Song dynasty|Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *[[:en:Lu You|Lu You]], a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *[[:en:Emperor Go-Hanazono|Emperor Go-Hanazono]], the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *[[:en:Lê Uy Mục|Lê Uy Mục]], the eighth king of the later [[:en:Lê dynasty|Lê dynasty]] of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *[[:en:Yi Gwang-sik|Yi Gwang-sik]], a Korean politician and general during the [[:en:Joseon dynasty|Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''
