revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk]][[en:Layue]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍t]][[ja:12月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 12월]][[th:เดือน 12]][[vi:Tháng chạp]][[wuu:腊月]][[za:Ndwenlab]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十二月]][[zh:农历十二月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk]][[en:Layue]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍t]][[ja:12月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 12월]][[th:เดือน 12]][[vi:Tháng chạp]][[wuu:腊月]][[za:Ndwenlab]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十二月]][[zh:农历十二月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk]][[en:Layue]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍t]][[ja:12月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 12월]][[th:เดือน 12]][[vi:Tháng chạp]][[wuu:腊月]][[za:Ndwenlab]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十二月]][[zh:农历十二月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q905470]] Wikidata name: 12th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar December|qq=}} '''Lunisolar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}} and {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5372133}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō calendar|en|Tenpō calendar|WD=Q1066717|lt=Tenpō Calendar}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Dahan (solar term)|en|Dahan (solar term)|WD=Q1325358|lt=Great Cold}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|en|December|WD=Q126|lt=December}} to early {{ill|February|en|February|WD=Q109|lt=February}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigenkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 言海|1=Genkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630981}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|en|Mutsuki|WD=Q6944038|lt=Mutsuki}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Iroha Jiruishō|en|Iroha Jiruishō|WD=Q11614525|lt=Shokuyojijirishu}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015|qq=}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Etymology|1=folk etymology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109964}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Là|1=Là|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612428}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref>
== Shiwasu Dates == {| class="wikitable" |+ Shiwasu Dates ! Gregorian Year !! New Moon !! Last Day !! Number of Days |- | 2014 || 2015th year {{ill|January 20|en|January 20|WD=Q2267|lt=January 20}} || 2015th year {{ill|February 18|en|February 18|WD=Q2343|lt=February 18}} || 30 days |- | 2015 || 2016th year {{ill|January 10|en|January 10|WD=Q2243|lt=January 10}} || 2016th year {{ill|February 7|en|February 7|WD=Q2322|lt=February 7}} || 29 days |- | 2016 || {{ill|December 29|en|December 29|WD=Q2862|lt=December 29}} || 2017th year {{ill|January 27|en|January 27|WD=Q2284|lt=January 27}} || 30 days |- | 2017 || 2018th year {{ill|January 17|en|January 17|WD=Q2264|lt=January 17}} || 2018th year {{ill|February 15|en|February 15|WD=Q2334|lt=February 15}} || 30 days |- | 2018 || 2019th year {{ill|January 6|en|January 6|WD=Q2221|lt=January 6}} || 2019th year {{ill|February 4|en|February 4|WD=Q2318|lt=February 4}} || 30 days |- | 2019 || {{ill|December 26|en|December 26|WD=Q2761|lt=December 26}} || 2020th year {{ill|January 24|en|January 24|WD=Q2278|lt=January 24}} || 30 days |- | 2020 || 2021st year {{ill|January 13|en|January 13|WD=Q2255|lt=January 13}} || 2021st year {{ill|February 11|en|February 11|WD=Q2335|lt=February 11}} || 30 days |- | 2021 || 2022nd year {{ill|January 3|en|January 3|WD=Q2156|lt=January 3}} || 2022nd year {{ill|January 31|en|January 31|WD=Q2289|lt=January 31}} || 29 days |- | 2022 || {{ill|December 23|en|December 23|WD=Q2676|lt=December 23}} || 2023rd year {{ill|January 21|en|January 21|WD=Q2273|lt=January 21}} || 30 days |- | 2023 || 2024th year {{ill|January 11|en|January 11|WD=Q2247|lt=January 11}} || 2024th year {{ill|February 9|en|February 9|WD=Q2324|lt=February 9}} || 30 days |- | 2024 || {{ill|December 31|en|December 31|WD=Q2912|lt=December 31}} || 2025th year {{ill|January 28|en|January 28|WD=Q2285|lt=January 28}} || 29 days |- | 2025 || 2026th year {{ill|January 19|en|January 19|WD=Q2266|lt=January 19}} || 2026th year {{ill|February 16|en|February 16|WD=Q2342|lt=February 16}} || 29 days |}
== References == <references />
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|Japanese language|url=https://www.sanseido-publ.co.jp/publ/gen/gen2lang/nazenanij/pdf/sample386-387.pdf|chapter=“Shiwasu” – Didn’t the Teacher Run?!|author=Kenichi Sekine|title=Nazenani Nihongo|date=2015-05-25|publisher=Sanseido|isbn=978-4-385-36606-7|pages=386–387|format=PDF|ref={{SfnRef|Sanseido|2015|qq=}}|qq=}}

== Related Articles == * {{ill|Laba Festival|en|Laba Festival|WD=Q2086945|lt=Laba Festival}} {{Month (Calendar)|Lunisolar December|List of Lunisolar Dates|Month (Lunisolar)|12|Day (Lunisolar)|{{ill|December 30 (Lunisolar)|ja|12月30日 (旧暦)|lt=30|lt_ja=30|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 30 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|qq=}}
{{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} {{short description|Month of the Chinese calendar|qq=}} {{more citations needed |date=June 2024|qq=}} {{italic title|qq=}}
'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月|qq=}}) is the last month of the year in the {{ill|Chinese calendar|en|Chinese calendar|WD=Q134032|lt=Chinese calendar}}. In general, the {{ill|Dahan (solar term)|en|Dahan (solar term)|WD=Q1325358|lt=Great Cold}}, the 24th {{ill|Solar term|en|Solar term|WD=Q1190993|lt=Solar term}}, is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month|qq=}}|qq=}}.
==Festival== *The {{ill|Laba Festival|en|Laba Festival|WD=Q2086945|lt=Laba Festival}} is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily|qq=}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日|qq=}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年|qq=}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕|qq=}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[:en:Aisin-Gioro Puyi|Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th {{ill|Qing dynasty|en|Qing dynasty|WD=Q8733|lt=Qing}} emperor of {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China}}, issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *{{ill|Emperor Go-Uda|en|Emperor Go-Uda|WD=Q440538|lt=Emperor Go-Uda}}, the 91st {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=emperor}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *{{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, the founder and first ''{{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}}'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *{{ill|Uemura Masahisa|en|Uemura Masahisa|WD=Q5966773|lt=Uemura Masahisa}}, a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *{{ill|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|en|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|WD=Q8249526|lt=Imperial Noble Consort Shushen}}, an Imperial concubine of the {{ill|Tongzhi Emperor|en|Tongzhi Emperor|WD=Q318811|lt=Tongzhi Emperor}}, ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *{{ill|Yue Fei|en|Yue Fei|WD=Q334398|lt=Yue Fei}}, Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[:en:Southern Song dynasty|Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *{{ill|Lu You|en|Lu You|WD=Q1069914|lt=Lu You}}, a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *{{ill|Emperor Go-Hanazono|en|Emperor Go-Hanazono|WD=Q451926|lt=Emperor Go-Hanazono}}, the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *{{ill|Lê Uy Mục|en|Lê Uy Mục|WD=Q842218|lt=Lê Uy Mục}}, the eighth king of the later {{ill|Lê dynasty|en|Lê dynasty|WD=Q10800789|lt=Lê dynasty}} of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *{{ill|Yi Gwang-sik|en|Yi Gwang-sik|WD=Q497153|lt=Yi Gwang-sik}}, a Korean politician and general during the [[:en:Joseon dynasty|Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk]][[en:Layue]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍t]][[ja:12月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 12월]][[th:เดือน 12]][[vi:Tháng chạp]][[wuu:腊月]][[za:Ndwenlab]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十二月]][[zh:农历十二月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk]][[en:Layue]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍t]][[ja:12月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 12월]][[th:เดือน 12]][[vi:Tháng chạp]][[wuu:腊月]][[za:Ndwenlab]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十二月]][[zh:农历十二月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 12 nguŏk]][[en:Layue]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 12-ngie̍t]][[ja:12月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 12월]][[th:เดือน 12]][[vi:Tháng chạp]][[wuu:腊月]][[za:Ndwenlab]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 12 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:十二月]][[zh:农历十二月]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q905470]] Wikidata name: 12th month [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Lunisolar December|qq=}} '''Lunisolar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}} and {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5372133}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō calendar|en|Tenpō calendar|WD=Q1066717|lt=Tenpō Calendar}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Dahan (solar term)|en|Dahan (solar term)|WD=Q1325358|lt=Great Cold}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|en|December|WD=Q126|lt=December}} to early {{ill|February|en|February|WD=Q109|lt=February}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigenkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 言海|1=Genkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630981}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|en|Mutsuki|WD=Q6944038|lt=Mutsuki}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Iroha Jiruishō|en|Iroha Jiruishō|WD=Q11614525|lt=Shokuyojijirishu}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015|qq=}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Etymology|1=folk etymology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109964}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Là|1=Là|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612428}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref>

== Related Articles == * {{ill|Laba Festival|en|Laba Festival|WD=Q2086945|lt=Laba Festival}} {{Month (Calendar)|Lunisolar December|List of Lunisolar Dates|Month (Lunisolar)|12|Day (Lunisolar)|{{ill|December 30 (Lunisolar)|ja|12月30日 (旧暦)|lt=30|lt_ja=30|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 30 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|qq=}}
{{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} {{short description|Month of the Chinese calendar|qq=}} {{more citations needed |date=June 2024|qq=}} {{italic title|qq=}}
'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月|qq=}}) is the last month of the year in the {{ill|Chinese calendar|en|Chinese calendar|WD=Q134032|lt=Chinese calendar}}. In general, the {{ill|Dahan (solar term)|en|Dahan (solar term)|WD=Q1325358|lt=Great Cold}}, the 24th {{ill|Solar term|en|Solar term|WD=Q1190993|lt=Solar term}}, is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month|qq=}}|qq=}}.
==Festival== *The {{ill|Laba Festival|en|Laba Festival|WD=Q2086945|lt=Laba Festival}} is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily|qq=}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日|qq=}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年|qq=}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕|qq=}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[:en:Aisin-Gioro Puyi|Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th {{ill|Qing dynasty|en|Qing dynasty|WD=Q8733|lt=Qing}} emperor of {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China}}, issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *{{ill|Emperor Go-Uda|en|Emperor Go-Uda|WD=Q440538|lt=Emperor Go-Uda}}, the 91st {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=emperor}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *{{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, the founder and first ''{{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}}'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *{{ill|Uemura Masahisa|en|Uemura Masahisa|WD=Q5966773|lt=Uemura Masahisa}}, a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *{{ill|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|en|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|WD=Q8249526|lt=Imperial Noble Consort Shushen}}, an Imperial concubine of the {{ill|Tongzhi Emperor|en|Tongzhi Emperor|WD=Q318811|lt=Tongzhi Emperor}}, ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *{{ill|Yue Fei|en|Yue Fei|WD=Q334398|lt=Yue Fei}}, Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[:en:Southern Song dynasty|Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *{{ill|Lu You|en|Lu You|WD=Q1069914|lt=Lu You}}, a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *{{ill|Emperor Go-Hanazono|en|Emperor Go-Hanazono|WD=Q451926|lt=Emperor Go-Hanazono}}, the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *{{ill|Lê Uy Mục|en|Lê Uy Mục|WD=Q842218|lt=Lê Uy Mục}}, the eighth king of the later {{ill|Lê dynasty|en|Lê dynasty|WD=Q10800789|lt=Lê dynasty}} of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *{{ill|Yi Gwang-sik|en|Yi Gwang-sik|WD=Q497153|lt=Yi Gwang-sik}}, a Korean politician and general during the [[:en:Joseon dynasty|Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

{{Lunisolar December|qq=}} '''Lunisolar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}} and {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5372133}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō calendar|en|Tenpō calendar|WD=Q1066717|lt=Tenpō Calendar}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Dahan (solar term)|en|Dahan (solar term)|WD=Q1325358|lt=Great Cold}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|en|December|WD=Q126|lt=December}} to early {{ill|February|en|February|WD=Q109|lt=February}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigenkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 言海|1=Genkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630981}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|en|Mutsuki|WD=Q6944038|lt=Mutsuki}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Iroha Jiruishō|en|Iroha Jiruishō|WD=Q11614525|lt=Shokuyojijirishu}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015|qq=}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Etymology|1=folk etymology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109964}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Là|1=Là|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612428}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref>

'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月|qq=}}) is the last month of the year in the {{ill|Chinese calendar|en|Chinese calendar|WD=Q134032|lt=Chinese calendar}}. In general, the {{ill|Dahan (solar term)|en|Dahan (solar term)|WD=Q1325358|lt=Great Cold}}, the 24th {{ill|Solar term|en|Solar term|WD=Q1190993|lt=Solar term}}, is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month|qq=}}|qq=}}.
==Festival== *The {{ill|Laba Festival|en|Laba Festival|WD=Q2086945|lt=Laba Festival}} is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily|qq=}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日|qq=}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年|qq=}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕|qq=}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[:en:Aisin-Gioro Puyi|Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th {{ill|Qing dynasty|en|Qing dynasty|WD=Q8733|lt=Qing}} emperor of {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China}}, issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *{{ill|Emperor Go-Uda|en|Emperor Go-Uda|WD=Q440538|lt=Emperor Go-Uda}}, the 91st {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=emperor}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *{{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, the founder and first ''{{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}}'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *{{ill|Uemura Masahisa|en|Uemura Masahisa|WD=Q5966773|lt=Uemura Masahisa}}, a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *{{ill|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|en|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|WD=Q8249526|lt=Imperial Noble Consort Shushen}}, an Imperial concubine of the {{ill|Tongzhi Emperor|en|Tongzhi Emperor|WD=Q318811|lt=Tongzhi Emperor}}, ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *{{ill|Yue Fei|en|Yue Fei|WD=Q334398|lt=Yue Fei}}, Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[:en:Southern Song dynasty|Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *{{ill|Lu You|en|Lu You|WD=Q1069914|lt=Lu You}}, a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *{{ill|Emperor Go-Hanazono|en|Emperor Go-Hanazono|WD=Q451926|lt=Emperor Go-Hanazono}}, the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *{{ill|Lê Uy Mục|en|Lê Uy Mục|WD=Q842218|lt=Lê Uy Mục}}, the eighth king of the later {{ill|Lê dynasty|en|Lê dynasty|WD=Q10800789|lt=Lê dynasty}} of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *{{ill|Yi Gwang-sik|en|Yi Gwang-sik|WD=Q497153|lt=Yi Gwang-sik}}, a Korean politician and general during the [[:en:Joseon dynasty|Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

{{Lunisolar December|qq=}} '''Lunisolar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}} and {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5372133}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō calendar|en|Tenpō calendar|WD=Q1066717|lt=Tenpō Calendar}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Dahan (solar term)|en|Dahan (solar term)|WD=Q1325358|lt=Great Cold}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|en|December|WD=Q126|lt=December}} to early {{ill|February|en|February|WD=Q109|lt=February}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigenkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 言海|1=Genkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630981}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|en|Mutsuki|WD=Q6944038|lt=Mutsuki}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Iroha Jiruishō|en|Iroha Jiruishō|WD=Q11614525|lt=Shokuyojijirishu}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015|qq=}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Etymology|1=folk etymology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109964}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Là|1=Là|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612428}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref>

== Related Articles == * {{ill|Laba Festival|en|Laba Festival|WD=Q2086945|lt=Laba Festival}} {{Month (Calendar)|Lunisolar December|List of Lunisolar Dates|Month (Lunisolar)|12|Day (Lunisolar)|{{ill|December 30 (Lunisolar)|ja|12月30日 (旧暦)|lt=30|lt_ja=30|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 30 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|qq=}}
{{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} {{short description|Month of the Chinese calendar|qq=}} {{more citations needed |date=June 2024|qq=}} {{italic title|qq=}}
'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月|qq=}}) is the last month of the year in the {{ill|Chinese calendar|en|Chinese calendar|WD=Q134032|lt=Chinese calendar}}. In general, the {{ill|Dahan (solar term)|en|Dahan (solar term)|WD=Q1325358|lt=Great Cold}}, the 24th {{ill|Solar term|en|Solar term|WD=Q1190993|lt=Solar term}}, is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month|qq=}}|qq=}}.
==Festival== *The {{ill|Laba Festival|en|Laba Festival|WD=Q2086945|lt=Laba Festival}} is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily|qq=}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日|qq=}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年|qq=}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕|qq=}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[:en:Aisin-Gioro Puyi|Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th {{ill|Qing dynasty|en|Qing dynasty|WD=Q8733|lt=Qing}} emperor of {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China}}, issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *{{ill|Emperor Go-Uda|en|Emperor Go-Uda|WD=Q440538|lt=Emperor Go-Uda}}, the 91st {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=emperor}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *{{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, the founder and first ''{{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}}'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *{{ill|Uemura Masahisa|en|Uemura Masahisa|WD=Q5966773|lt=Uemura Masahisa}}, a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *{{ill|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|en|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|WD=Q8249526|lt=Imperial Noble Consort Shushen}}, an Imperial concubine of the {{ill|Tongzhi Emperor|en|Tongzhi Emperor|WD=Q318811|lt=Tongzhi Emperor}}, ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *{{ill|Yue Fei|en|Yue Fei|WD=Q334398|lt=Yue Fei}}, Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[:en:Southern Song dynasty|Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *{{ill|Lu You|en|Lu You|WD=Q1069914|lt=Lu You}}, a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *{{ill|Emperor Go-Hanazono|en|Emperor Go-Hanazono|WD=Q451926|lt=Emperor Go-Hanazono}}, the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *{{ill|Lê Uy Mục|en|Lê Uy Mục|WD=Q842218|lt=Lê Uy Mục}}, the eighth king of the later {{ill|Lê dynasty|en|Lê dynasty|WD=Q10800789|lt=Lê dynasty}} of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *{{ill|Yi Gwang-sik|en|Yi Gwang-sik|WD=Q497153|lt=Yi Gwang-sik}}, a Korean politician and general during the [[:en:Joseon dynasty|Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

{{Lunisolar December|qq=}} '''Lunisolar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}} and {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5372133}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō calendar|en|Tenpō calendar|WD=Q1066717|lt=Tenpō Calendar}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Dahan (solar term)|en|Dahan (solar term)|WD=Q1325358|lt=Great Cold}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|en|December|WD=Q126|lt=December}} to early {{ill|February|en|February|WD=Q109|lt=February}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigenkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 言海|1=Genkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630981}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|en|Mutsuki|WD=Q6944038|lt=Mutsuki}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Iroha Jiruishō|en|Iroha Jiruishō|WD=Q11614525|lt=Shokuyojijirishu}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015|qq=}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Etymology|1=folk etymology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109964}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Là|1=Là|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612428}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref>

== Related Articles == * {{ill|Laba Festival|en|Laba Festival|WD=Q2086945|lt=Laba Festival}} {{Month (Calendar)|Lunisolar December|List of Lunisolar Dates|Month (Lunisolar)|12|Day (Lunisolar)|{{ill|December 30 (Lunisolar)|ja|12月30日 (旧暦)|lt=30|lt_ja=30|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 30 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|qq=}}
{{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} {{short description|Month of the Chinese calendar|qq=}} {{more citations needed |date=June 2024|qq=}} {{italic title|qq=}}
'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月|qq=}}) is the last month of the year in the {{ill|Chinese calendar|en|Chinese calendar|WD=Q134032|lt=Chinese calendar}}. In general, the {{ill|Dahan (solar term)|en|Dahan (solar term)|WD=Q1325358|lt=Great Cold}}, the 24th {{ill|Solar term|en|Solar term|WD=Q1190993|lt=Solar term}}, is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month|qq=}}|qq=}}.
==Festival== *The {{ill|Laba Festival|en|Laba Festival|WD=Q2086945|lt=Laba Festival}} is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily|qq=}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日|qq=}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年|qq=}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕|qq=}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[:en:Aisin-Gioro Puyi|Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th {{ill|Qing dynasty|en|Qing dynasty|WD=Q8733|lt=Qing}} emperor of {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China}}, issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *{{ill|Emperor Go-Uda|en|Emperor Go-Uda|WD=Q440538|lt=Emperor Go-Uda}}, the 91st {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=emperor}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *{{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, the founder and first ''{{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}}'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *{{ill|Uemura Masahisa|en|Uemura Masahisa|WD=Q5966773|lt=Uemura Masahisa}}, a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *{{ill|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|en|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|WD=Q8249526|lt=Imperial Noble Consort Shushen}}, an Imperial concubine of the {{ill|Tongzhi Emperor|en|Tongzhi Emperor|WD=Q318811|lt=Tongzhi Emperor}}, ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *{{ill|Yue Fei|en|Yue Fei|WD=Q334398|lt=Yue Fei}}, Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[:en:Southern Song dynasty|Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *{{ill|Lu You|en|Lu You|WD=Q1069914|lt=Lu You}}, a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *{{ill|Emperor Go-Hanazono|en|Emperor Go-Hanazono|WD=Q451926|lt=Emperor Go-Hanazono}}, the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *{{ill|Lê Uy Mục|en|Lê Uy Mục|WD=Q842218|lt=Lê Uy Mục}}, the eighth king of the later {{ill|Lê dynasty|en|Lê dynasty|WD=Q10800789|lt=Lê dynasty}} of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *{{ill|Yi Gwang-sik|en|Yi Gwang-sik|WD=Q497153|lt=Yi Gwang-sik}}, a Korean politician and general during the [[:en:Joseon dynasty|Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

{{Lunisolar December|qq=}} '''Lunisolar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}} and {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5372133}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō calendar|en|Tenpō calendar|WD=Q1066717|lt=Tenpō Calendar}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Dahan (solar term)|en|Dahan (solar term)|WD=Q1325358|lt=Great Cold}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|en|December|WD=Q126|lt=December}} to early {{ill|February|en|February|WD=Q109|lt=February}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigenkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 言海|1=Genkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630981}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|en|Mutsuki|WD=Q6944038|lt=Mutsuki}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Iroha Jiruishō|en|Iroha Jiruishō|WD=Q11614525|lt=Shokuyojijirishu}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015|qq=}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Etymology|1=folk etymology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109964}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Là|1=Là|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612428}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref>

'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月|qq=}}) is the last month of the year in the {{ill|Chinese calendar|en|Chinese calendar|WD=Q134032|lt=Chinese calendar}}. In general, the {{ill|Dahan (solar term)|en|Dahan (solar term)|WD=Q1325358|lt=Great Cold}}, the 24th {{ill|Solar term|en|Solar term|WD=Q1190993|lt=Solar term}}, is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month|qq=}}|qq=}}.
==Festival== *The {{ill|Laba Festival|en|Laba Festival|WD=Q2086945|lt=Laba Festival}} is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily|qq=}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日|qq=}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年|qq=}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕|qq=}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[:en:Aisin-Gioro Puyi|Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th {{ill|Qing dynasty|en|Qing dynasty|WD=Q8733|ltq=Qing}} emperor of {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China}}, issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *{{ill|Emperor Go-Uda|en|Emperor Go-Uda|WD=Q440538|lt=Emperor Go-Uda}}, the 91st {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=emperor}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *{{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, the founder and first ''{{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}}'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *{{ill|Uemura Masahisa|en|Uemura Masahisa|WD=Q5966773|lt=Uemura Masahisa}}, a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *{{ill|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|en|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|WD=Q8249526|lt=Imperial Noble Consort Shushen}}, an Imperial concubine of the {{ill|Tongzhi Emperor|en|Tongzhi Emperor|WD=Q318811|lt=Tongzhi Emperor}}, ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *{{ill|Yue Fei|en|Yue Fei|WD=Q334398|lt=Yue Fei}}, Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[:en:Southern Song dynasty|Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *{{ill|Lu You|en|Lu You|WD=Q1069914|lt=Lu You}}, a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *{{ill|Emperor Go-Hanazono|en|Emperor Go-Hanazono|WD=Q451926|lt=Emperor Go-Hanazono}}, the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *{{ill|Lê Uy Mục|en|Lê Uy Mục|WD=Q842218|lt=Lê Uy Mục}}, the eighth king of the later {{ill|Lê dynasty|en|Lê dynasty|WD=Q10800789|lt=Lê dynasty}} of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *{{ill|Yi Gwang-sik|en|Yi Gwang-sik|WD=Q497153|lt=Yi Gwang-sik}}, a Korean politician and general during the [[:en:Joseon dynasty|Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

{{Lunisolar December|qq=}} '''Lunisolar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}} and {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5372133}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō calendar|en|Tenpō calendar|WD=Q1066717|lt=Tenpō Calendar}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Dahan (solar term)|en|Dahan (solar term)|WD=Q1325358|lt=Great Cold}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|en|December|WD=Q126|lt=December}} to early {{ill|February|en|February|WD=Q109|lt=February}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigenkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 言海|1=Genkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630981}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|en|Mutsuki|WD=Q6944038|lt=Mutsuki}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Iroha Jiruishō|en|Iroha Jiruishō|WD=Q11614525|lt=Shokuyojijirishu}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015|qq=}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Etymology|1=folk etymology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109964}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Là|1=Là|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612428}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref>

== Related Articles == * {{ill|Laba Festival|en|Laba Festival|WD=Q2086945|lt=Laba Festival}} {{Month (Calendar)|Lunisolar December|List of Lunisolar Dates|Month (Lunisolar)|12|Day (Lunisolar)|{{ill|December 30 (Lunisolar)|ja|12月30日 (旧暦)|lt=30|lt_ja=30|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 30 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|qq=}}
{{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} {{short description|Month of the Chinese calendar|qq=}} {{more citations needed |date=June 2024|qq=}} {{italic title|qq=}}
'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月|qq=}}) is the last month of the year in the {{ill|Chinese calendar|en|Chinese calendar|WD=Q134032|lt=Chinese calendar}}. In general, the {{ill|Dahan (solar term)|en|Dahan (solar term)|WD=Q1325358|lt=Great Cold}}, the 24th {{ill|Solar term|en|Solar term|WD=Q1190993|lt=Solar term}}, is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month|qq=}}|qq=}}.
==Festival== *The {{ill|Laba Festival|en|Laba Festival|WD=Q2086945|lt=Laba Festival}} is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily|qq=}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日|qq=}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年|qq=}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕|qq=}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[:en:Aisin-Gioro Puyi|Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th {{ill|Qing dynasty|en|Qing dynasty|WD=Q8733|lt=Qing}} emperor of {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China}}, issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *{{ill|Emperor Go-Uda|en|Emperor Go-Uda|WD=Q440538|lt=Emperor Go-Uda}}, the 91st {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=emperor}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *{{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, the founder and first ''{{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun}}'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *{{ill|Uemura Masahisa|en|Uemura Masahisa|WD=Q5966773|lt=Uemura Masahisa}}, a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *{{ill|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|en|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|WD=Q8249526|lt=Imperial Noble Consort Shushen}}, an Imperial concubine of the {{ill|Tongzhi Emperor|en|Tongzhi Emperor|WD=Q318811|lt=Tongzhi Emperor}}, ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *{{ill|Yue Fei|en|Yue Fei|WD=Q334398|lt=Yue Fei}}, Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[:en:Southern Song dynasty|Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *{{ill|Lu You|en|Lu You|WD=Q1069914|lt=Lu You}}, a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *{{ill|Emperor Go-Hanazono|en|Emperor Go-Hanazono|WD=Q451926|lt=Emperor Go-Hanazono}}, the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *{{ill|Lê Uy Mục|en|Lê Uy Mục|WD=Q842218|lt=Lê Uy Mục}}, the eighth king of the later {{ill|Lê dynasty|en|Lê dynasty|WD=Q10800789|lt=Lê dynasty}} of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *{{ill|Yi Gwang-sik|en|Yi Gwang-sik|WD=Q497153|lt=Yi Gwang-sik}}, a Korean politician and general during the [[:en:Joseon dynasty|Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

{{Lunisolar December|qq=}} '''Lunisolar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China (region)|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}} and {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar|1=Lunisolar calendar}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5372133}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō calendar|en|Tenpō calendar|WD=Q1066717|lt=Tenpō Calendar|1=Tenpō calendar}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Dahan (solar term)|en|Dahan (solar term)|WD=Q1325358|lt=Great Cold|1=Dahan (solar term)}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|en|December|WD=Q126|lt=December|1=December}} to early {{ill|February|en|February|WD=Q109|lt=February|1=February}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar|1=Lunisolar calendar}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigenkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 言海|1=Genkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630981}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|en|Mutsuki|WD=Q6944038|lt=Mutsuki|1=Mutsuki}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Iroha Jiruishō|en|Iroha Jiruishō|WD=Q11614525|lt=Shokuyojijirishu|1=Iroha Jiruishō}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015|qq=}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Etymology|1=Folk etymology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109964}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Là|1=Q11612428|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612428}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref>

{{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} {{short description|Month of the Chinese calendar|qq=}} {{more citations needed |date=June 2024|qq=}} {{italic title|qq=}}
'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月|qq=}}) is the last month of the year in the {{ill|Chinese calendar|en|Chinese calendar|WD=Q134032|lt=Chinese calendar|1=Chinese calendar}}. In general, the {{ill|Dahan (solar term)|en|Dahan (solar term)|WD=Q1325358|lt=Great Cold|1=Dahan (solar term)}}, the 24th {{ill|Solar term|en|Solar term|WD=Q1190993|lt=Solar term|1=Solar term}}, is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month|qq=}}|qq=}}.
==Festival== *The {{ill|Laba Festival|en|Laba Festival|WD=Q2086945|lt=Laba Festival|1=Laba Festival}} is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily|qq=}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日|qq=}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年|qq=}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕|qq=}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[:en:Aisin-Gioro Puyi|Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th {{ill|Qing dynasty|en|Qing dynasty|WD=Q8733|lt=Qing|1=Qing dynasty}} emperor of {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China|1=China}}, issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *{{ill|Emperor Go-Uda|en|Emperor Go-Uda|WD=Q440538|lt=Emperor Go-Uda|1=Emperor Go-Uda}}, the 91st {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=emperor|1=Emperor of Japan}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *{{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, the founder and first ''{{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}}'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *{{ill|Uemura Masahisa|en|Uemura Masahisa|WD=Q5966773|lt=Uemura Masahisa|1=Uemura Masahisa}}, a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *{{ill|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|en|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|WD=Q8249526|lt=Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|1=Imperial Noble Consort Shushen}}, an Imperial concubine of the {{ill|Tongzhi Emperor|en|Tongzhi Emperor|WD=Q318811|lt=Tongzhi Emperor|1=Tongzhi Emperor}}, ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *{{ill|Yue Fei|en|Yue Fei|WD=Q334398|lt=Yue Fei|1=Yue Fei}}, Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[:en:Southern Song dynasty|Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *{{ill|Lu You|en|Lu You|WD=Q1069914|lt=Lu You|1=Lu You}}, a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *{{ill|Emperor Go-Hanazono|en|Emperor Go-Hanazono|WD=Q451926|lt=Emperor Go-Hanazono|1=Emperor Go-Hanazono}}, the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *{{ill|Lê Uy Mục|en|Lê Uy Mục|WD=Q842218|lt=Lê Uy Mục|1=Lê Uy Mục}}, the eighth king of the later {{ill|Lê dynasty|en|Lê dynasty|WD=Q10800789|lt=Lê dynasty|1=Lê dynasty}} of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *{{ill|Yi Gwang-sik|en|Yi Gwang-sik|WD=Q497153|lt=Yi Gwang-sik|1=Yi Gwang-sik}}, a Korean politician and general during the [[:en:Joseon dynasty|Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

{{Lunisolar December|qq=}} '''Lunisolar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China (region)|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}} and {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar|1=Lunisolar calendar}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5372133}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō calendar|en|Tenpō calendar|WD=Q1066717|lt=Tenpō Calendar|1=Tenpō calendar}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Dahan (solar term)|en|Dahan (solar term)|WD=Q1325358|lt=Great Cold|1=Dahan (solar term)}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|en|December|WD=Q126|lt=December|1=December}} to early {{ill|February|en|February|WD=Q109|lt=February|1=February}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar|1=Lunisolar calendar}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigenkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 言海|1=Genkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630981}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|en|Mutsuki|WD=Q6944038|lt=Mutsuki|1=Mutsuki}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Iroha Jiruishō|en|Iroha Jiruishō|WD=Q11614525|lt=Shokuyojijirishu|1=Iroha Jiruishō}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015|qq=}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Etymology|1=Folk etymology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109964}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Là|1=Q11612428|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612428}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref>

{{Lunisolar December|qq=}} '''Lunisolar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China (region)|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q29520}} and {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar|1=Lunisolar calendar}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5372133}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō calendar|en|Tenpō calendar|WD=Q1066717|lt=Tenpō Calendar|1=Tenpō calendar}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Dahan (solar term)|en|Dahan (solar term)|WD=Q1325358|lt=Great Cold|1=Dahan (solar term)}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|en|December|WD=Q126|lt=December|1=December}} to early {{ill|February|en|February|WD=Q109|lt=February|1=February}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|WD=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar|1=Lunisolar calendar}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigenkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 言海|1=Genkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11630981}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|en|Mutsuki|WD=Q6944038|lt=Mutsuki|1=Mutsuki}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Iroha Jiruishō|en|Iroha Jiruishō|WD=Q11614525|lt=Shokuyojijirishu|1=Iroha Jiruishō}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015|qq=}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Etymology|1=Folk etymology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109964}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. Additionally, it is called “Ratsuki” (臘月), derived from the month of {{ill|Là|ja|臘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Là|1=Q11612428|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11612428}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=行事目白押しの12月？ |url=https://www.j-cfa.com/report/columu/20221201_09/ |website=公益社団法人 日本中国友好協会（日中友好協会） |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=臘月(ろうげつ)とは？ 意味や使い方 |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%87%98%E6%9C%88-662563 |website=コトバンク |access-date=2024-03-21 |language=ja|qq=}}</ref>

{{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} {{short description|Month of the Chinese calendar|qq=}} {{more citations needed |date=June 2024|qq=}} {{italic title|qq=}}
'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月|qq=}}) is the last month of the year in the {{ill|Chinese calendar|en|Chinese calendar|WD=Q134032|lt=Chinese calendar|1=Chinese calendar}}. In general, the {{ill|Dahan (solar term)|en|Dahan (solar term)|WD=Q1325358|lt=Great Cold|1=Dahan (solar term)}}, the 24th {{ill|Solar term|en|Solar term|WD=Q1190993|lt=Solar term|1=Solar term}}, is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month|qq=}}|qq=}}.
==Festival== *The {{ill|Laba Festival|en|Laba Festival|WD=Q2086945|lt=Laba Festival|1=Laba Festival}} is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily|qq=}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日|qq=}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年|qq=}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕|qq=}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[:en:Aisin-Gioro Puyi|Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th {{ill|Qing dynasty|en|Qing dynasty|WD=Q8733|lt=Qing|1=Qing dynasty}} emperor of {{ill|China|en|China|WD=Q148|lt=China|1=China}}, issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *{{ill|Emperor Go-Uda|en|Emperor Go-Uda|WD=Q440538|lt=Emperor Go-Uda|1=Emperor Go-Uda}}, the 91st {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=emperor|1=Emperor of Japan}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *{{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|WD=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, the founder and first ''{{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|WD=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}}'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *{{ill|Uemura Masahisa|en|Uemura Masahisa|WD=Q5966773|lt=Uemura Masahisa|1=Uemura Masahisa}}, a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *{{ill|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|en|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|WD=Q8249526|lt=Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|1=Imperial Noble Consort Shushen}}, an Imperial concubine of the {{ill|Tongzhi Emperor|en|Tongzhi Emperor|WD=Q318811|lt=Tongzhi Emperor|1=Tongzhi Emperor}}, ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *{{ill|Yue Fei|en|Yue Fei|WD=Q334398|lt=Yue Fei|1=Yue Fei}}, Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[:en:Southern Song dynasty|Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *{{ill|Lu You|en|Lu You|WD=Q1069914|lt=Lu You|1=Lu You}}, a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *{{ill|Emperor Go-Hanazono|en|Emperor Go-Hanazono|WD=Q451926|lt=Emperor Go-Hanazono|1=Emperor Go-Hanazono}}, the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *{{ill|Lê Uy Mục|en|Lê Uy Mục|WD=Q842218|lt=Lê Uy Mục|1=Lê Uy Mục}}, the eighth king of the later {{ill|Lê dynasty|en|Lê dynasty|WD=Q10800789|lt=Lê dynasty|1=Lê dynasty}} of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *{{ill|Yi Gwang-sik|en|Yi Gwang-sik|WD=Q497153|lt=Yi Gwang-sik|1=Yi Gwang-sik}}, a Korean politician and general during the [[:en:Joseon dynasty|Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''

{{Lunisolar December|qq=}} '''Lunisolar December''' (旧暦十二月) is the twelfth month of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} used in {{ill|China|ja|中国|1=China (region)|2=ja|3=中国|lt=China|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/China|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q29520}} and {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, based on the {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|qid=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar|1=Lunisolar calendar}}.
In calendars such as the {{ill|Japanese Calendar|ja|和暦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Calendar|1=Japanese year|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5372133}} prior to the {{ill|Tenpō calendar|en|Tenpō calendar|qid=Q1066717|lt=Tenpō Calendar|1=Tenpō calendar}}, December is defined as the month that includes {{ill|Dahan (solar term)|en|Dahan (solar term)|qid=Q1325358|lt=Great Cold|1=Dahan (solar term)}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}}, it corresponds approximately to late {{ill|December|en|December|qid=Q126|lt=December|1=December}} to early {{ill|February|en|February|qid=Q109|lt=February|1=February}}. In the Eastern {{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|qid=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar|1=Lunisolar calendar}}, because the number of days in a month, known as {{ill|Large and Small|ja|大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Large and Small|1=Large and Small|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10935019}} (with large months having 30 days and small months 29 days), varies by year, there are years when the final day of the month is the 29th.
== Alternate Names and Aliases == ;Etymology of “Shiwasu” December’s alternate name is '''Shiwasu''' (しはす, しわす). The origin of “shiwasu” is unknown due to its antiquity. The {{ill|Daigenkai|ja|大言海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigenkai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 言海|1=Genkai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11630981}} states, “It is said to be an abbreviation of ‘Toshihatsu’ (トシハツ), or that it signifies a month in which all matters come to fruition (シハ), as well as the time when agricultural activities conclude,” and also remarks, “The names of the twelve months are assigned according to the progression of the ripening of rice” (see the {{ill|Mutsuki|en|Mutsuki|qid=Q6944038|lt=Mutsuki|1=Mutsuki}} entry). Furthermore, there is a theory from the Heian period that attributes the term to the busy movement of monks (with “shi” meaning monk) attending Buddhist ceremonies, as mentioned in {{ill|Iroha Jiruishō|en|Iroha Jiruishō|qid=Q11614525|lt=Shokuyojijirishu|1=Iroha Jiruishō}}; however, this is regarded as a folk etymology lacking linguistic basis, and by the Heian period the true origin of “shiwasu” had already been lost. {{Sfn|Sanseido|2015|qq=}}<ref>高島俊男：お言葉ですが・・・（７）、漢字語源の筋ちがい、p.86、文春文庫、第1刷、2006th year 6月10日、（株）文藝春秋</ref> (see also {{ill|Folk Etymology|ja|民間語源#日本語における民間語源|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Etymology|1=Folk etymology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109964}}).
;Other It is also referred to by the alias “Kitō” (季冬) in reference to lunar December. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Laba Festival|en|Laba Festival|qid=Q2086945|lt=Laba Festival|1=Laba Festival}} {{Month (Calendar)|Lunisolar December|List of Lunisolar Dates|Month (Lunisolar)|12|Day (Lunisolar)|{{ill|December 30 (Lunisolar)|ja|12月30日 (旧暦)|lt=30|lt_ja=30|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 30 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}|qq=}}
{{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} {{short description|Month of the Chinese calendar|qq=}} {{more citations needed |date=June 2024|qq=}} {{italic title|qq=}}
'''''Làyuè''''' ({{zh|s=腊月|qq=}}) is the last month of the year in the {{ill|Chinese calendar|en|Chinese calendar|qid=Q134032|lt=Chinese calendar|1=Chinese calendar}}. In general, the {{ill|Dahan (solar term)|en|Dahan (solar term)|qid=Q1325358|lt=Great Cold|1=Dahan (solar term)}}, the 24th {{ill|Solar term|en|Solar term|qid=Q1190993|lt=Solar term|1=Solar term}}, is in ''Làyuè''. The name comes from the winter sacrifice, just as February. In Japan, the month known as {{nihongo|Shiwasu|師走||{{lit|priests' busiest month|qq=}}|qq=}}.
==Festival== *The {{ill|Laba Festival|en|Laba Festival|qid=Q2086945|lt=Laba Festival|1=Laba Festival}} is ''Layue'' 8.<ref name="chinadaily">{{Cite web |last=CGTN |date=January 12, 2024 |title=Layue, a joyful celebration in the run-up to Spring Festival |url=https://www.chinadaily.com.cn/a/202401/12/WS65a09b0da3105f21a507bf3a.html |access-date=12 June 2024 |website=China Daily|qq=}}</ref> The original definition of the Laba festival was the day of the winter sacrifice ({{zh|t=臘日|s=腊日|qq=}}), and the date is the third ''Wùrì'' after the Winter Solstice. *The Preliminary Eve ({{zh|s=小年|qq=}}) is ''Layue'' 23. *The New Year's Eve ({{zh|s=除夕|qq=}}) is the last day of the year, ''Layue'' 29 or 30.
==Events== [[:en:Aisin-Gioro Puyi|Aisin-Gioro Puyi]], the 12th {{ill|Qing dynasty|en|Qing dynasty|qid=Q8733|lt=Qing|1=Qing dynasty}} emperor of {{ill|China|en|China|qid=Q148|lt=China|1=China}}, issued the imperial abdication edict on ''Layue'' 25, 1911.
==Births== *{{ill|Emperor Go-Uda|en|Emperor Go-Uda|qid=Q440538|lt=Emperor Go-Uda|1=Emperor Go-Uda}}, the 91st {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=emperor|1=Emperor of Japan}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1269 *{{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, the founder and first ''{{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|qid=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}}'' of the [[Tokugawa shogunate]] of Japan, ''Shiwasu'' 26, 1542 *{{ill|Uemura Masahisa|en|Uemura Masahisa|qid=Q5966773|lt=Uemura Masahisa|1=Uemura Masahisa}}, a Japanese Christian pastor, theologian and critic of Meiji and Taishō periods, ''Shiwasu'' 1, 1857 *{{ill|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|en|Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|qid=Q8249526|lt=Imperial Noble Consort Shushen|1=Imperial Noble Consort Shushen}}, an Imperial concubine of the {{ill|Tongzhi Emperor|en|Tongzhi Emperor|qid=Q318811|lt=Tongzhi Emperor|1=Tongzhi Emperor}}, ''Làyuè'' 1, 1859
==Deaths== *{{ill|Yue Fei|en|Yue Fei|qid=Q334398|lt=Yue Fei|1=Yue Fei}}, Yue Yun, Zhang Xian, military generals who lived in the [[:en:Southern Song dynasty|Southern Song dynasty]], ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1141'' *{{ill|Lu You|en|Lu You|qid=Q1069914|lt=Lu You|1=Lu You}}, a prominent poet of China's Southern Song dynasty, ''Làyuè 29<sub>th</sub> 1209'' *{{ill|Emperor Go-Hanazono|en|Emperor Go-Hanazono|qid=Q451926|lt=Emperor Go-Hanazono|1=Emperor Go-Hanazono}}, the 102nd emperor of Japan, ''Shiwasu 27<sub>th</sub> 1470'' *{{ill|Lê Uy Mục|en|Lê Uy Mục|qid=Q842218|lt=Lê Uy Mục|1=Lê Uy Mục}}, the eighth king of the later {{ill|Lê dynasty|en|Lê dynasty|qid=Q10800789|lt=Lê dynasty|1=Lê dynasty}} of Vietnam, ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1509'' *{{ill|Yi Gwang-sik|en|Yi Gwang-sik|qid=Q497153|lt=Yi Gwang-sik|1=Yi Gwang-sik}}, a Korean politician and general during the [[:en:Joseon dynasty|Joseon dynasty]], ''Làyuè 1<sub>st</sub> 1563''
